task: T31 — Test coverage for parsers/builders/NPC/vehicle/minigame logic (tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/* new files only; TEST-ONLY, no production changes)
baseBehaviors: MD3 (Mod_IDP3_Load, Base/darkplaces/model_alias.c:1578): magic 'IDP3', version MUST be 15 (check at model_alias.c:1594; MD3VERSION in model_alias.h:126). Header 108B; frameinfo 56B (mins/maxs/origin/radius/name[16]); tag 112B (name[64]+origin+3x3 rotation matrix), stored FRAME-MAJOR (all tags of frame 0, then frame 1...); mesh sub-header 108B starting with its own 'IDP3' ident and NO version; mesh lump offsets relative to the MESH struct start; vertex 8B = short xyz scaled by 1/64 (model_alias.c:294) + lat/long byte normal decoded as (cos(yaw)sin(pitch), sin(yaw)sin(pitch), cos(pitch)) with byte*2pi/256 (mod_md3_sin table)

DPM (Mod_DARKPLACESMODEL_Load, model_alias.c:2163): id 'DARKPLACESMODEL\0' (16B incl NUL); type must be 2 (skeletal); EVERY multi-byte field is BIG-endian (BigLong/BigFloat throughout, same pattern as model_alias.c:1796-1811); header/per-mesh/per-frame offsets are ABSOLUTE (file-relative); bone 40B name[32]+parent+flags, mesh 56B, frame 68B with ofs_bonepositions -> numbones 3x4 float matrices (48B each); bone-relative weighted verts (dpmbonevert_t 32B)

IQM (Mod_INTERQUAKEMODEL_Load, model_alias.c:3219): magic 'INTERQUAKEMODEL\0' 16B, version 1 OR 2; header 124B (char[16]+27 uint32), all section offsets file-relative, little-endian; v1 joints 44B store quat xyz only — w reconstructed w = -sqrt(max(0, 1-x^2-y^2-z^2)) as DP does; v2 joints 48B; POSITION(float3)+TEXCOORD(float2) required, NORMAL/TANGENT/BLENDINDEXES/BLENDWEIGHTS/COLOR optional, other arrays ignored; triangles validated < numVerts; pose channels decoded from ushort framedata stream via per-channel offset/scale

Sprites (Mod_IDSP_Load, model_sprite.c:267): magic IDSP -> version 1 (Quake, 8-bit external palette), 2 (Half-Life), 32 (spr32, BGRA->RGBA swap); HL palette colour count MUST be 256 else Host_Error (model_sprite.c:~314); HL rendermode palettes (model_sprite.c:319-362): SPRHL_OPAQUE/ADDITIVE full alpha 255, SPRHL_INDEXALPHA colour = palette entry 765-767 with alpha = index, SPRHL_ALPHATEST pal[255] = 0; unknown rendermode Host_Error; frames walk SPR_SINGLE(0)/SPR_GROUP(1) slots, groups = N intervals then N dspriteframe_t {origin[2],width,height} (Mod_Sprite_SharedSetup); IDS2 .sp2 (Mod_IDS2_Load model_sprite.c:381): version must be 2, frame 80B = w,h,origin_x,origin_y,name[64], left = -origin[0] (sign opposite spr), sprite type FORCED to SPR_VP_PARALLEL

Q3BSP (model_brush.c): header = 'IBSP' + version + 17 lump_t{fileofs,filelen} (Q3HEADER_LUMPS); LoadEntities :5151 (text lump, COM_ParseToken_Simple, parsebackslash=false so literal backslash kept, comments skipped); LoadTextures :5218 stride 72 = name[64]+surfaceflags+contents, Host_Error if lump len not a multiple; LoadVertices :5414 stride 44 = origin3f+normal3f+texcoord2f+lightmap2f+color4ub, color4ub converted to float /255; LoadFaces :5806 stride 104 (12 header ints + 14-int union), unknown face type is TOLERATED with a DPrint (:5854) not an error, patchsize must be valid (:5929); brushsides 8B (12B with surfaceflags in v48 IG, :5298); external lightmaps lm_NNNN when lump 14 empty (:5590-5608)

Turret lifecycle (Base qcsrc/common/turrets/sv_turrets.qc): turret_damage :207 — DEAD_DEAD early-return; !this.active takes NO damage; SAME_TEAM -> damage *= autocvar_g_friendlyfire else return when friendlyfire is 0; TFL_DMG_HEADSHAKE jolts head angles by (random()-0.5)*damage; TUR_FLAG_MOVE adds vforce to velocity; health<=0 -> event_damage func_null + think=turret_die at time. turret_die :166 — DEAD_DEAD, SOLID_NOT, takedamage NO, health set 0; TFL_DMG_DEATH_NORESPAWN -> delete head+self, else turret_hide :159 (EF_NODRAW, nextthink = time + respawntime - 0.2 -> turret_respawn). turret_respawn :263 — restores SOLID_BBOX, DAMAGE_AIM, health = max_health, head angles = idle_aim, avelocity zeroed. turret_initparams :1182 defaults: respawntime 60, shot_refire 1 (bound .01..9999), shot_dmg = refire*50, shot_radius = dmg*0.5, shot_speed 2500, shot_spread 0.0125, shot_force = dmg*0.5+radius*0.5, shot_volly 1, volly_refire = refire*volly (<=60), target_range = speed*0.5, range_min = radius*2, range_optimal = range*0.5, aim_maxrot 90, aim_maxpitch 20, aim_speed 36, firetolerance 5+radius*2, all 5 select biases 1 (bound -10..10), ammo_max = dmg*10, ammo_recharge = dmg*0.5

Turret aiming (sv_turrets.qc:12-62 turret_aim_generic): TFL_AIM_SIMPLE -> real_origin(enemy); TFL_AIM_LEAD -> + enemy.velocity * mintime where mintime = max(attack_finished-time,0)+sys_frametime; +TFL_AIM_SHOTTIMECOMPENSATE -> impact_time = dist/shot_speed, lead by (impact_time+mintime); +TFL_AIM_ZPREDICT integrates gravity per sys_frametime step for airborne WALK/STEP/TOSS/BOUNCE targets; TFL_AIM_SPLASH traces down 32->-64 and aims at floor hit

Add a tiny shared fixture helper inside each new test file (NOT a shared production file): private const string DataDir = @"C:\Users\Bryan\Projects\Xonotic\XonoticGodot\assets\data"; skip pattern `if (!Directory.Exists(DataDir)) return;` + `using var vfs = new VirtualFileSystem(); vfs.MountGameDir(DataDir);` exactly as AssetParserTests does; cache expensive parses (zombie.dpm, atelier.bsp) in a `static readonly Lazy<T>` so the serial suite stays fast

2. Parser wave A — binary readers vs real assets + synthetic error paths: Md3ReaderTests, DpmReaderTests, IqmReaderTests, SpriteReaderTests (each: real-asset layout invariants per the DP behaviors above; synthetic in-memory byte buffers for magic/version/truncation/bounds errors that run with or without assets)

3. Parser wave B — BSP + text parsers: BspReaderLayoutTests (real map: IBSP v46, 17 validated lumps, texture stride 72 names, vertex/face counts consistent, worldspawn first entity, node/leaf child ranges; synthetic: out-of-range lump dir throws, version 45 throws), EntityLumpParserTests (pure text: quotes, // and /* */ comments, backslash NOT unescaped, duplicate-key-last-wins, stray-token tolerance, _-prefixed key preserved), Q3ShaderParserDirectiveTests (synthetic shaders: surfaceparm trans sets ONLY translucent — the rebirth-trans-lightmap-grates regression pin; blendFunc/rgbGen wave/tcMod scroll+stretch/skyParms incl '-'/deformVertexes; unknown directive -> onWarning callback, no throw), BezierPatchTests (flat patch coplanarity, exact corner interpolation, analytic quadratic-arc midpoint, no NaN on degenerate grids, triangle-count growth with tessellation level)

4. Parser wave C — sidecars: SkinFileTests (replace + Q3 CSV + tag lines, last-write-wins, nodraw, extension kept verbatim), FrameGroupsTests (4-token line, fps default 20, loop default true, optional 5th name token, comment lines; plus a real .framegroups via vfs.Find if present), ModelSoundsTests (id/path/variant parse, //TAG banner, // disabled lines, VariantCount 0 => IsSingle)

5. Turrets (largest zero-coverage area, 4 classes): TurretMathTests (AngleMods/ShortAngle wrap identities, ShortAngleVxy, AngleOfs, steering SteerPull normalization/SteerArrive slowdown — pure, no harness); TurretTargetingTests (flat-floor Boot harness: ValidTarget gates — dead/same-team/range-min/range-max/LOS per turret_validate_target; ScoreTarget formula vs sv_turrets.qc bias math incl d_score = min(opt,dist)/max(opt,dist); SelectTarget picks max-score); TurretAimTrackTests (AimPoint: SIMPLE = target origin; LEAD+SHOTTIMECOMPENSATE = origin + vel*(dist/shot_speed + mintime); ZPREDICT gravity integration matches the per-sys_frametime loop; Track slew rate clamped by aim_speed*dt and aim_maxrot/maxpitch); TurretLifecycleTests (spawn ewheel/plasma via TurretSpawnFuncs -> health 200/respawntime 30 from turrets.cfg; Damage: friendlyfire-0 same-team no-op, inactive no-op, DEAD_DEAD no-op, headshake under TFL_DMG_HEADSHAKE; kill -> Die: Solid.Not/TakeDamage No/health 0; Respawn restores max health/Solid.BBox/idle aim; Use adopts activator team)

6. Monsters (2 classes): MonsterFrameworkTests (HomeProjectile bends velocity toward target at turnRate and accel/decel toward speedMax, returns false past deathTime; ChainedZaps hits radius entities once each; DropItem spawns loot entity with pop-up velocity + classname from the loot string; AddFireDamage applies Burning and deals ~totalDamage over burnTime; CheckDanger flags lava ahead); MonsterAttackTests (Boot harness + per-monster cvar pins: zombie melee 55 dmg after 1s delay / leap speed 500 force 55; spider web applies Webbed for damagetime 7 and projectile speed 1300; mage spike homing + push radius 150 chance gate (seed Prandom); wyvern fireball spawns projectile that ignites Burning; golem lightning ChainedZaps wiring — each asserts against monsters.cfg values, NOT current-code magic numbers)

7. 8. Minigames (5 classes): PegSolitaireTests (32 pegs + centre hole start; legal jump removes jumped peg; non-jump/diagonal/occupied rejected; 1-peg-left => Win; no-jumps-left => Draw flag per PS_TURN_DRAW); NineMensMorrisTests (7 pieces each; place->move phase shift; mill => Take, prefer non-mill unless TakeAny; 3 pieces => Fly; sub-3 or stalemate => Win); BulldozerTests (DirFromName mapping; dozer single-step move; boulder push only into empty valid tile; bd_check_winner => Win; LoadLevel placements; editor activate/place/close transitions); PushPullTests (ValidTile, push and pull box moves, win detection, illegal-move rejection); SnakeTests (deterministic rng injection: step advance, growth+score on food, wall/self death, 180-reversal ignored, Tick accumulates StepInterval — explicitly commented as pinning ORIGINAL port behavior, no QC source); plus extend the manager angle inside these classes by driving each game via MinigameSessionManager.ForwardMove('move <tile>') so the cmd-string contract is covered per game; AI legality: extend TTT AI (never occupied/out-of-turn over a scripted sequence) and Pong AI paddle stays inside court bounds over Tick

filesToModify: NONE required. Optional narrow extensions (allowed by the task charter 'extend existing tests'): tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/MinigameSessionTests.cs (add TTT-AI full-legality sweep + Pong-AI bounds facts) — preferable to put these in the new per-game files instead to keep the diff additive-only
chokepointSnippets: No chokepoint changes needed. This task creates new test files only; it does not require edits to GameWorld.cs, ServerNet.cs, ClientNet.cs, NetProtocol.cs, Commands.cs, DamageSystem.cs, MapObjectsRegistry.cs, or Registries.cs. Tests consume DamageSystem/Registries strictly through the existing public seams already used by MonsterDamageDeathTests (GameInit.Boot(services) -> Combat.Damage / Registry<T> / MutatorHooks) and MinigameSessionTests (GameWorld.Boot("dm") read-only usage for the command verb). If a turret/monster default is found to disagree with the shipped cfg while writing TurretLifecycleTests/MonsterAttackTests, the fix would land in the OWNING file (e.g. a turret def under Gameplay/Turrets/) — that is outside this task: write the test pinning current behavior with a FLAGGED doc-comment and report the exact file:line + cfg line in the wave report instead of editing
risks: Turret code has NO live caller (verified: no TurretAI/TurretSpawn references in src/XonoticGodot.Server or .Engine) — these tests exercise ~9k LOC for the first time and may surface real bugs (cfg-default drift like the already-fixed MonsterState.RespawnTime 10-vs-20, or unexercised math). Per the charter: pin current behavior, flag in doc-comments, report — do not fix production code

Snake is an ORIGINAL implementation (Snake.cs:3-7; zero QC source — verified no 'snake' in Base minigames). Its tests pin port-local behavior only; they must say so explicitly or a future fidelity audit will mistake them for Base parity claims

Global static state: MutatorHooks chains, MonsterAI counters, Minigames registry, Prandom, Api.Services are process-global. Every new harness must reset them in the ctor (copy the VehicleRuntimeTests/MonsterDamageDeathTests preamble) and carry [Collection("GlobalState")]; never re-enable parallelism (TestParallelization.cs comment explains why)

Asset-dependent tests silently no-op when assets/data is missing (the established skip-if-missing pattern returns, it does not Skip) — coverage on a clean CI machine is lower than locally; keep every error-path/synthetic assertion asset-free so the always-on half stays meaningful

Fixture parse cost: zombie.dpm is 3MB and atelier.bsp is large; parsing per-[Fact] in a serial suite adds seconds. Use static Lazy<T> caches per test class (or the smaller wyvern_lod2.dpm / _init.bsp / g_h1_luma.iqm fixtures) to keep the suite under its current sub-minute budget

MD3 normal decode: the port uses MathF.Sin/Cos directly while DP uses the mod_md3_sin lookup table — values can differ in the last ulp; assert unit-length and direction with tolerance (1e-3), never bit-exactness

Pitch sign convention: VecToAngles/AngleVectors pitch signs are intentionally inverse (QMath memory) — turret/vehicle aim tests must compare via QMath/FixedVecToAngles round-trips, not hand-derived angle signs

Turret Track/AimPoint use Api.Clock.FrameTime and trace services — tests must drive a real SimulationLoop or set the clock via the EngineServices facade as BotPerfBench does (reflection on GameClock), otherwise slew-rate assertions are time-flaky

Mage push attack has a 0.7 chance gate (monsters.cfg:31) and turret headshake uses random() — seed Prandom (Prandom.Seed(0xC0FFEE) pattern from VehicleRuntimeTests) before any randomized assertion; if a code path uses System.Random instead of the seeded PRNG, that itself is a reportable determinism finding

The working tree carries the uncommitted player-loop parity pass — Entity/Player field semantics (IsCorpse, DeadState, regen fields) match disk, not git HEAD; new tests compile against disk state and may conflict if that pass is reverted
testPlan: Build + run everything new: `dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests` from C:/Users/Bryan/Projects/Xonotic/XonoticGodot (Windows dotnet, NOT WSL — per rebirth-csharp-build). Expect current baseline (~1059+ tests) plus the new classes, zero failures, runtime still well under a minute
Targeted runs while developing: `dotnet test tests/XonoticGodot.Tests --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Md3ReaderTests"` (and per-class equivalents) to iterate without the full serial suite

Skip-guard verification: temporarily rename XonoticGodot/assets/data -> data.bak, re-run the suite — every asset-backed test must pass (no-op), every synthetic test must still assert; rename back and confirm the asset-backed assertions actually execute (add a cheap sentinel assertion count or run with -l detailed once)
Determinism check: run the full suite twice back-to-back; any diff implicates a missed static reset or unseeded randomness (the suite is serial, so flakiness = leaked global state)

Cross-check three pinned constants by hand against Base before merging: turrets.cfg:12-13 (ewheel 200/30), monsters.cfg:6-7 (zombie melee 55/1), vehicles.cfg:135-139 (racer 100/200) — these are the canary values guarding cfg-vs-port drift
Confirm no production file changed: `git status` under XonoticGodot must show only added files under tests/XonoticGodot.Tests/ (plus the pre-existing uncommitted player-loop files untouched)
